st have
1. The word Tikanika must be a vernacular name for 'small.' Cf. Kanna^a
word, cikani, in this sense.
2. Under B.J. XX. 10.
3. B. S. XUV. 14, 18; XLVII1. 22, 29.
4. B.J. XXIV. 16; XXVIII. 3.
5. Vide India As seen in Br Samhila, p. 32.

Ittiroduction XV
revised them. For, in P.S.XV.10 he refers to Sathhita\ in B. J.
XX VI11.6 to F. Palala and Samhitd in the past tense.
Many other works are also attributed to the Acarya. Al-Biruni
states that Varaha wrote Hordpancahotiya and a work on architecture.
The latter must be the chapters LIII, LVI and LVII of B.S. that
deal with this subject.
Vafakanikd seems to be a work on portents. 120 verses of this
work arc quoted in Ballalasena's Adbhutasdgara as those of Varaha-
mihira. He is supposed to have written works on erotics and poetics
too. Another work in manuscript entitled Paheapaksi is attributed to
our author. It is a short work on divination through the five vowels
3r, ^ ^ % and aft. It has a commentary too. Other works attributed
